purpose: First AS-owned schematic key for one triangular RLEM/Universal Cell node, paired with one executable Universal Cell transition trace.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel on the triangular node.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel on the triangular node.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel on the triangular node.

layers.summary: A right-memory wire node rotates an incoming three-channel signal from input tuple positions 0, 1, 2 to output positions 1, 2, 0.
layers.boundary: This layer states only symbolic RLEM-style signal redirection and memory orientation; it does not assert physical circulator behavior.

layers.summary: The node is represented as a triangular GELC/RLEM schematic key with north, east, and west ports.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes port orientation and wire geometry as schematic vocabulary, not as a full GELC circuit proof.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one fixed-role Universal Cell activation.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the existing AS Universal Cell probe and does not add new transition behavior.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware remains a candidate interpretation of the node, not verified AS hardware.
layers.boundary: This layer explicitly marks physical implementation as unverified and keeps RALA-like auxiliary reconfiguration separate.
